_comment: Hand-written prose for docs/api-reference.md. Keys are fully-qualified method names (bare for the root database, namespaced for plugins). Each entry may have: summary (string), args (object of name->description), example (string). Run `npm run gen:api-reference` after editing.

publish.summary: Publish a new message to the local feed using the current identity, signing and appending it to the log.

args.content: the message content object; must include a `type` string

add.summary: Append an already-formed, validly-signed message to the local store without re-signing it. Used when ingesting messages from other feeds.

get.summary: Fetch a single message by its key (message id).

args.key: the message id (`%...sha256`), or an options object

del.summary: Delete a message or feed from the local store. Destructive and local-only; it does not unpublish anything that has already been replicated to peers.

queue.summary: Queue a raw message for validation and storage without immediately flushing it to disk.

flush.summary: Flush any queued writes, calling back once they are durably stored.

search.summary: Low-level full-store search hook exposed by the database. Most callers should prefer `messagesByType` or `links`.

createLogStream.summary: Stream messages in the order the local node received them. Supports `limit`, `reverse`, `live`, `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte`, and `keys`/`values` options.

createFeedStream.summary: Stream messages ordered by their claimed `timestamp`. Honors `gt`/`gte`/`lt`/`lte` range filters on the timestamp, plus `limit`, `reverse`, and `live`.

createHistoryStream.summary: Stream the messages of a single feed in sequence order, optionally starting from a given sequence. This is the core replication read used over the wire.

args.live: keep the stream open for new messages

createUserStream.summary: Stream one feed's messages much like `createHistoryStream`, with the log-stream option shape (`limit`, `reverse`, `gt`/`lt` on sequence).

createWriteStream.summary: Sink that accepts messages and adds each one to the local store, the streaming counterpart of `add`.

createSequenceStream.summary: Stream the local store's overall sequence as it advances; useful for tracking write progress.

messagesByType.summary: Stream every message of a given `type` across all known feeds. This is the primary index used by Decent and ssbski to build their feeds.

links.summary: Traverse link relationships between messages and feeds (e.g. who mentioned a message). Filter by `source`, `dest`, `rel`, and `values`.

latest.summary: Stream the latest sequence for every feed in the local database (not only followed feeds), emitting `{ id, sequence, ts }` records.

getLatest.summary: Get the latest message for a single feed.

latestSequence.summary: Get the latest known sequence number (and timestamp) for a feed without returning the whole message.

getVectorClock.summary: Return a map of every known feed to its latest sequence — a vector clock of local replication state.

getAtSequence.summary: Fetch the message at a specific `[feedId, sequence]` position.

whoami.summary: Return the current local identity as `{ id }`.

ready.summary: Report whether the database has finished its initial load and is ready to serve reads.

progress.summary: Return indexing/replication progress counters, used to drive the startup progress bar.

status.summary: Return a snapshot of runtime status, including database and (when available) local-peer state.

version.summary: Return the running server version (the package version).

help.summary: Return inline help describing the methods available on the root object.

plugins.install.summary: Install a user plugin from npm into the configuration directory and stream installation output. Restart is required for it to load.

plugins.uninstall.summary: Remove a previously installed user plugin, streaming the npm output.

plugins.enable.summary: Mark an installed user plugin as enabled so it loads on the next start.

plugins.disable.summary: Mark an installed user plugin as disabled so it is skipped on the next start.

plugins.help.summary: Return inline help for the `plugins` namespace.

gossip.add.summary: Register a peer address in the gossip table so the server may connect to it.

gossip.remove.summary: Remove a peer from the gossip table.

gossip.connect.summary: Connect immediately to a peer by multiserver address.

gossip.disconnect.summary: Drop an existing connection to a peer.

gossip.changes.summary: Stream gossip table change events as peers are added, connected, or dropped.

gossip.reconnect.summary: Reset connections so the server retries peers from scratch.

gossip.disable.summary: Disable a gossip transport or behavior at runtime.

gossip.enable.summary: Enable a gossip transport or behavior at runtime.

gossip.ping.summary: Duplex keep-alive used between connected peers to measure liveness and clock skew.

gossip.get.summary: Look up the gossip table entry for a specific peer.

gossip.peers.summary: Return the current list of known peers and their connection state.

gossip.help.summary: Return inline help for the `gossip` namespace.

replicate.request.summary: Historically, ask the replicator to (stop) replicating a feed. Replication here is handled by `ebt`.

replicate.block.summary: Historically, mark a feed as blocked from replication. Retained for compatibility; blocking is expressed through the social graph.

ebt.replicate.summary: Epidemic broadcast tree replication: the duplex stream peers use to exchange feed updates efficiently.

ebt.request.summary: Tell EBT to start or stop replicating a given feed.

ebt.block.summary: Tell EBT to refuse replicating a feed for a given peer relationship.

ebt.peerStatus.summary: Return EBT's view of replication progress for each peer and feed.

friends.hopStream.summary: Stream changes to the hop-distance graph as follows and blocks are processed.

friends.onEdge.summary: Subscribe to individual follow/block edge changes in the social graph.

friends.isFollowing.summary: Report whether one feed follows another.

friends.isBlocking.summary: Report whether one feed blocks another.

friends.hops.summary: Return a map of feed id to hop distance from the local identity (0 = self, 1 = followed, etc.), the set used to decide what to replicate.

friends.get.summary: Return the raw follow/block graph as nested `{ source: { dest: state } }` maps.

friends.createFriendStream.summary: Stream feed ids within a hop range, optionally live, as the graph grows.

friends.stream.summary: Stream the full hops state and subsequent updates as a single live view.

friends.help.summary: Return inline help for the `friends` namespace.

blobs.get.summary: Stream the bytes of a blob by its hash id.

blobs.getSlice.summary: Stream a byte range of a blob, given `{ key, start, end }`.

blobs.add.summary: Sink that stores incoming bytes as a content-addressed blob and calls back with its hash. The CLI `blobs.add` wrapper feeds a file or stdin into it.

blobs.rm.summary: Remove a blob from the local store.

blobs.ls.summary: Stream the hashes of all blobs currently held locally.

blobs.has.summary: Report whether one or more blobs are present locally.

args.hash: a blob id or array of ids

blobs.size.summary: Return the byte size of a locally-held blob, or null if absent.

blobs.meta.summary: Return metadata about the blob store or a specific blob.

blobs.want.summary: Register interest in a blob so the server fetches it from peers that have it, calling back when it arrives.

blobs.push.summary: Actively push a blob out to peers rather than waiting for them to want it.

blobs.changes.summary: Stream notifications as blobs are added to the local store.

blobs.createWants.summary: Stream the current want-list — blobs this node is seeking — used during peer exchange.

blobs.help.summary: Return inline help for the `blobs` namespace.

invite.create.summary: Create an invite code that another peer can redeem to connect to and be followed by this server.

args.opts: a number of uses, or an options object such as `{ uses, modern }`

invite.use.summary: Server side of redemption: accept a redeeming peer, follow them, and confirm the invite.

invite.accept.summary: Client side of redemption: connect to the inviting pub using an invite code, request access, and publish a follow.

git.create.summary: Create a new git repository feed message, returning the repo id used by the git-over-SSB / git-over-HTTP server.

query.read.summary: Unsupported compatibility method. This fails with an explicit error in SQLite mode; use `messagesByType` or root `links` instead.

query.explain.summary: Return the index and plan a given query would use, for debugging query selection.

query.help.summary: Return inline help for the `query` namespace.

links2.read.summary: Stream link relationships between messages and feeds with filtering on `source`, `dest`, and `rel`. This is the working link index in this build (`links` on the root delegates to the same data).

args.query: a links query, e.g. `{ source, dest, rel, values: true }`

links2.help.summary: Return inline help for the `links2` namespace.

ooo.stream.summary: Out-of-order message stream. This build ships a stub, so the stream yields nothing.

ooo.get.summary: Fetch an out-of-order message by id. Stubbed in this build.

ooo.help.summary: Return inline help for the `ooo` namespace.
